Container.Name: StandardNotes-LocalStack
Container.Overview:
LocalStack companion container for the Standard Notes self-hosted backend on Unraid. Provides the SNS / SQS endpoints that the upstream standardnotes/server image expects to find at "localstack:4566".
REQUIRED companion for the official Standard Notes server image — it mirrors the upstream docker-compose.example.yml. Without it, StandardNotesServer's worker logs (/var/lib/server/logs/files-worker.log) repeat "SQSError: SQS receive message failed: getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND localstack" and background jobs fail in a loop, destabilising sync. Skip this template ONLY if you have explicit alternative SNS / SQS endpoints configured upstream.
HOSTNAME RESOLUTION — THE SERVER MUST RESOLVE "localstack": The official standardnotes/server image resolves the literal hostname "localstack" (port 4566). The container name in this template is "StandardNotes-LocalStack" for clarity in the Unraid UI, but the StandardNotesServer container MUST still be able to resolve the bare name "localstack". Two working setups: • USER-DEFINED DOCKER NETWORK (shared bridge): put StandardNotesServer and this container on the SAME custom bridge and give this container the network alias "localstack" (Unraid: Advanced → Network alias). Docker's embedded DNS (127.0.0.11) will then resolve the name. • br0 / macvlan / VLAN / STATIC-IP install (each container has its own fixed IP on br0.<vlan>, which bypasses Docker DNS): install this container on the SAME VLAN as StandardNotesServer and assign it a FIXED IP (e.g. 192.168.x.x). Then, in StandardNotesServer's Extra Parameters, APPEND: --add-host=localstack:<LocalStack-IP> (substitute the IP you assigned above). Restart StandardNotesServer for the change to take effect. Without this mapping, getent hosts localstack from StandardNotesServer will return empty and files-worker.log will fill with "getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND localstack". Verify from the Unraid host: docker exec StandardNotesServer getent hosts localstack Output must be non-empty before connecting any client.
REQUIRED INIT SCRIPT — SNS TOPICS / SQS QUEUES: LocalStack starts empty. The official standardnotes/server image expects a fixed set of SNS topics and SQS queues to exist (eg. auth-local-queue, syncing-server-local-queue, files-local-queue and friends). Upstream provides docker/localstack_bootstrap.sh which creates them; this template ships the same script at scripts/localstack_bootstrap.sh in the GitHub repo. Mount it at /etc/localstack/init/ready.d/localstack_bootstrap.sh — LocalStack runs everything under that directory automatically when it becomes ready, exactly the way upstream docker-compose does it.
On the Unraid host, BEFORE first start: mkdir -p /mnt/user/appdata/standardnotes curl -fsSL -o /mnt/user/appdata/standardnotes/localstack_bootstrap.sh \ https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junkerderprovinz/standardnotes-server/main/scripts/localstack_bootstrap.sh chmod +x /mnt/user/appdata/standardnotes/localstack_bootstrap.sh Then leave the "LocalStack Bootstrap Script" path mapping below pointing at that file. Without this, LocalStack is reachable on TCP 4566 but has no topics/queues — standardnotes/server workers will loop on missing-queue errors, account creation can hang, and notes can duplicate.
Notes: • Restricted to SERVICES=sns,sqs to keep the footprint small. • Port 4566 is exposed on the bridge network so the server container can reach it; you do NOT need to publish it to the host. • The container is started with --hostname=localstack so its own kernel hostname matches the name upstream code expects. That alone is not enough for cross-container resolution — see the HOSTNAME RESOLUTION block above for what DNS / extra-hosts has to be in place on the StandardNotesServer side.

Config.Name: LocalStack Edge Port
Config.Description: Edge port that exposes the SNS / SQS endpoints. Reachable from the Standard Notes server container on the same bridge network. Publishing to the host is optional.

Config.Name: Services (SERVICES)
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of LocalStack services to start. Standard Notes only uses SNS and SQS.

Config.Name: External Hostname (HOSTNAME_EXTERNAL)
Config.Description: Hostname LocalStack advertises to other containers. Leave at 'localstack' — the upstream standardnotes/server image hard-codes the hostname 'localstack' for SNS / SQS. To make the name actually resolve from StandardNotesServer, EITHER place both containers on the same user-defined Docker network with this container aliased as 'localstack', OR — on br0 / macvlan / VLAN / static-IP installs — install this container on the same VLAN with a fixed IP and APPEND `--add-host=localstack:<LocalStack-IP>` to StandardNotesServer's Extra Parameters. Verify with: docker exec StandardNotesServer getent hosts localstack (must return a non-empty line).

Config.Name: Log Level (LS_LOG)
Config.Description: LocalStack log level: trace, debug, info, warn, error.

Config.Name: LocalStack Bootstrap Script
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Init script that creates the SNS topics and SQS queues the official standardnotes/server image expects. LocalStack auto-runs anything in /etc/localstack/init/ready.d/ once it becomes ready. Before first start: download this repo's scripts/localstack_bootstrap.sh to the host path on the right and chmod +x it: `mkdir -p /mnt/user/appdata/standardnotes && curl -fsSL -o /mnt/user/appdata/standardnotes/localstack_bootstrap.sh https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junkerderprovinz/standardnotes-server/main/scripts/localstack_bootstrap.sh && chmod +x /mnt/user/appdata/standardnotes/localstack_bootstrap.sh`. Without it, LocalStack is reachable but empty — workers loop on missing queues and notes can duplicate. Verify after start with: `docker exec StandardNotes-LocalStack awslocal --endpoint-url=http://localhost:4566 sqs list-queues` and `sns list-topics`.
